pages.full_text:
We drove into St Tropez + parked the car quite far out + walked in. There were hoardes of people. We saw the museum. We had drinks in an expensive cafe by the sea front. Obscenely expensive boats. Then we walked in the old town + bought sandwiches

[glued-in postcard: painting reproduced on art card]

L'Annonciade Museum (in an old church)
Edouard Vuillard 1868-1940.
La Soupe d'Annette 1900-1901.
photo_captions: L'Annonciade Museum (in an old church) Edouard Vuillard 1868-1940. La Soupe d'Annette 1900-1901.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible at top-left; the corner is cropped/cut off in the scan. The text at the top continues from the previous spread and mid-sentence flows into the right page ('bought sandwiches' continues with 'which we ate seated on a window sill.' on the right page). A glued-in postcard reproducing a painting is the main feature of this page; the caption is written vertically in handwriting beside it: 'L'Annonciade Museum (in an old church) Edouard Vuillard 1868-1940. La Soupe d'Annette 1900-1901.' — this refers to the Musée de l'Annonciade in St Tropez. Year cannot be pinned from this page; context from nearby spreads suggests the trip is to the south of France.

pages.full_text:
which we ate seated on a window sill.
We drove to Gigaro beach - Heracles beach.
V. hot. We swam + had tea + then came back to the campsite. The farmer gave Simon a big bunch of grapes.
__Sunday__
   We went into Grimaud + were early for church. We looked around the town - very hilly, old path[s] we walked up near the castle. Attractive doorways + water fountains + pavemented roads. The church service was clear - I thought the priest must be English from the way he spoke french. The singing brought people in + was often in Latin.
   We drove on to a Geant supermarket which was very, very crowded. We got back to the campsite at 2 pm for lunch. Unfortunately Simon crunched his door into a tree while parking.
   It was very interesting to see how mass is in France, though I didn't understand anything from it. the music was very nice. The time we spent at the beach and in the sea was a lot of fun, the Views from the south of France, Saint Tropez, were amazing; I do miss the sea. I also enjoyed a[lot] the time I spent on the Swings. The food was [lucky/lovely], especially the chocolate cakes.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 60 at top-right. 'Sunday' is underlined as a day heading. The word 'swam' has a strikethrough over a previous word (appears to be 'sat' struck out before 'swam'). 'Geant' likely refers to a Géant Casino supermarket (French chain). 'Pavemented roads' — verbatim, unusual word. Near the end, 'a lot' reads as two words but is slightly compressed. 'The food was lucky' — possibly 'lovely'; difficult to read with certainty. 'The Swings' — capitalised in original, possibly a specific feature at the campsite. Year cannot be pinned from this page; could be 'Gigaro beach - Heracles beach' — Gigaro is near La Croix-Valmer, south of Saint Tropez. The narrator appears to be a child or teenager (mentions enjoying the swings, chocolate cakes, and riding in a VW campervan family trip).
